title: numset
text: numset Initialize the value of the specified counter. numset CounterName , InitialValue { , LibraryName } Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description CounterName String Name of a counter in LibraryName . InitialValue Number A numeric expression rounded down to an integer value -2,147,483,648 through 2,147,483,647 (that is, -2 31 through 2 31 -1 ). LibraryName String Name of the library to which CounterName belongs. If omitted, the library SYSTEM_LIBRARY is used. Libraries allow different projects to maintain their own set of counters. (The current library is available in $variation .) Return Values Table 2. Values returned in $status Value Meaning 0 CounterName was successfully initialized. -1 InitialValue was out of range, CounterName not defined, or Uniface was unable to access the Repository. Table 3. Values commonly returned by $procerror following numgen and numset Value Error constant Meaning -2 through -12 <UIOSERR_*> Errors during database I/O. -16 through -30 <UNETERR_*> Errors during network I/O. -1108 <UPROCERR_COUNTER> Uniface was unable to access the Repository or the counter is not defined. -1203 <UPROCERR_RANGE> Value out of range. The value of the increment or the generated counter is incorrect. Use Allowed in all component types except self-contained Report and Services. Description The numset statement initializes CounterName to the value InitialValue . The statement can be used to initialize counters that are used by the numgen statement. For more information, see Using Counters . Example: Resetting a Counter The following example resets the counter NUMBERCOUNTER in the library SALES_LIBRARY to 0: ; trigger: Detail numset "NUMBERCOUNTER", 0,"SALES_LIBRARY" commit "$UUU" message "Counter %%NUMBERCOUNTER in SALES_LIBRARY set to 0." Copy Related Links numgen Increment the value of the specified counter. $variation Return or specify the name of the library containing objects needed by the application or component.
